utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so jerry is a 45 -year-old male who came in today with an ankle injury jerry what happened

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hey doctor michael yeah so my son sean i think you met in the past he he started playing basketball and there we do multiple seasons and so we're kinda getting ready for the next season that starts in in april and we were at the courts plan and i went up for a shot and he's far more aggressive than with me than he is with you know his peers i guess he could say and so he he found me while i was going for a lap and then when i came down i kind of landed awkwardly and i kind of like rolled and twisted my my ankle so now it's it's hurting a bit like kind of on the outside you can you can even see it's it's bruised up and a bit swollen

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and yeah i've been having trouble walking and it just does n't feel solid really stable which is a problem i i recently got back into working out and i had been in a really good rhythm going to the gym

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and i do n't want to disrupt that moments on because it took a lot for me to get back to a good place so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: absolutely okay totally understand that glad to hear that you're back out on the court playing with your son how is he doing by the way

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: he is doing great he is doing great i mean you know he is a big step kurry fan so he likes to keep up shots from from way out past the three point line and he is only ten and tiny so it's it's not a good idea for him to do that but he is doing really well and i'm just happy he's tried every sport and basketball's really what took so i'm just really happy that he has a sport and loves and couple of his buddies playing to lead with him so it's just makes me happy that he's found something he really enjoys

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good good to hear alright well let's take a look at that ankle it looks like it's pretty swollen so let me just do a quick exam on that right now alright so looks like the outside of your ankle if i push on that does that hurt pretty bad

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah yes

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so exquisite tenderness tenderness laterally and then if i push here does that hurt too

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah a little bit

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so some tenderness over the medial deltoid region so swelling on the lateral side of the ankle no epidermolysis skin is intact looks like you have brisk capillary refill no horrible malalignment so alright can you can you stand on it did you say that you're having trouble walking at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i mean i can stand on it and i ca n't walk on it it just it hurts and it feels like i'm going to possibly injure it more just because it does n't feel particularly solid

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: sure so it does n't quite feel stable

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay okay i gotcha so i know you had an x-ray as you came in today and so i'm just looking at this x-ray here i'll show you on the screen right here i can turn my monitor towards you this is an ap lateral oblique and this is your right ankle so what we're looking at is a displaced lateral malleolus at the weber c level there's no evidence of medial or posterior malleolar fractures but this is a fracture on the lateral side of your ankle now based on the position it's a bit unstable that's why you're feeling some of that that instability when you're walking so for your diagnosis what i'm gon na put down is a lateral malleolar fracture and what i would recommend for that since it is in the location that it is is you're probably unfortunately gon na need surgery we're gon na wan na get that healed what that includes is putting some plate and some screws in and you're gon na be out for a little bit so i know you've been trying to work out and and you wan na get back on the court but but you may have to have to sit out for a little bit we'll get you some crutches

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: how long it's a little bit because i'm also i i forgot to say i'm also i did volunteer to coach

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: starting in april so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright well

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: how long

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah you you're probably gon na be out for about three months but continue the coaching go ahead and and let's get you back out there we'll get you some crutches and and hopefully you can kinda you know get back on the court start coaching and then within that three months we'll we'll get you back out doing some exercise again

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright in the meantime i'm gon na prescribe some medication for now let's try meloxicam and try to get some of the swelling down i want you to ice it and also keep that that ankle elevated do you have a job where you can elevate your ankle regularly

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i i work from home so that that should n't be a problem it's just everything else i do n't know who is gon na walk my dogs and and do all this stuff that's rest of my family refuses to walk my dog so we'll figure it out

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah i i'm a dog walker as well so alright sorry for the bad news but let's get you healed up so that we can get you back out doing everything you need to do

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: alright thank you doctor

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright thanks

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 45-year-old male presenting with a right ankle injury.
Right ankle injury
The doctor asks the patient to describe the circumstances of the injury.
The patient sustained a right ankle injury while playing basketball, landing awkwardly and twisting the ankle.
The patient reports lateral ankle pain, bruising, and swelling.
The patient reports difficulty walking and a feeling of instability in the ankle.
The patient recently resumed a consistent gym routine.
The doctor performs a physical examination of the right ankle.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences pain when pressure is applied to the lateral ankle.
The doctor notes exquisite lateral tenderness and tenderness over the medial deltoid region.
The doctor notes lateral swelling, intact skin, brisk capillary refill, and no malalignment.
The doctor asks if the patient is able to stand or walk on the ankle.
The patient can stand but has difficulty walking due to pain and instability.
X-rays show a displaced lateral malleolus fracture at the Weber C level with no medial or posterior fractures.
Surgical treatment
The doctor recommends open reduction internal fixation surgery.
The doctor will provide crutches for ambulation.
The patient asks about the expected recovery duration.
The patient is volunteering to coach basketball starting in April.
The estimated recovery time is three months before resuming exercise.
The doctor prescribes meloxicam to reduce swelling.
The doctor recommends icing and elevating the ankle.
The doctor asks if the patient's job allows for regular ankle elevation.
The patient works from home and can elevate his ankle.
